quranicRelevance: Relying on God (Tawakkul), trustee/guardian (Wakeel), and entrusting affairs.

keyDerivations.meaning: Trustee, guardian, protector, or deputy (attribute of God)
To rely, trust, or place one's confidence
To entrust, or appoint as a guardian

figurativeOrRareUsages: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الوَكِيلُ: God is sufficient for us, and how excellent a Trustee/Guardian He is.
تَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ: Place your reliance and trust in God.
discardedNoise: Specific details of agency contracts in post-classical commercial law.
Obscure details on camels relying on their drivers.
summaryEn: The root denotes trust and guardianship, undergirding the spiritual duty of reliance on God (Tawakkul) and His role as the ultimate Trustee (Wakeel).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الأمانة والتوكيل، مؤصلاً للواجب الإيماني بالاعتماد على الله (التوكل) وصفته سبحانه كوكيل حافظ خبير.
rawText: إِوْتَكَلَ 8 see 5. === مُتَّكَلٌ is used as an inf. n. of اِتَّكَلَ. تَوَاكَلَهُ 6 He deserted him, or it: i. q. تركته: see two exs. voce سَدِرٌ. تَوَكَّلَ عَلَيْهِ 5 , and 🡻اِتَّكَلَعليه, He relied upon him; (S, Msb;) and confided in him: (Msb:) he submitted himself to him. (K.) === تَوَكَّلَ بِالأَمْرِ He became responsible to him for the management of the affair. (TA.) === تَوَكَّلَ لَهُ بِهِ He became responsible to him for it. (TA.) === تَوَكَّلَ فِى أَمْرٍ He became وَكِيل in an affair. === توكّل بِمَالِ أَحَدٍ He became administrator of one's property. تُكَلَةٌ : see وَكَلٌ وَكَالَةٌ , for دَارُ الوَكَالَةِ, A factory: pl. وَكَائِلُ. وَكَلٌ and 🡻وُكَلَةٌ and 🡻تُكَلَةٌ An impotent man, (S, K,) who commits his affair to another. (S.) وَكَلَهُ إِلَى رَأْيِهِ 1 , inf. n. وَكْلٌ and وُكُولٌ, He left him to his opinion, or judgment. (TA.) And وَكَلْتُهُ إِلَى نَفْسِهِ, aor. يَكِلُ, inf. n. وُكُولٌ, [I left him to himself;] I did not manage his affair, nor aid him. (Msb.) And كِلْنِى إِلَى كَذَا Leave thou me to manage such a thing. (TA.) === وَكَلْتُهُ إِلَى دِينِهِ [I left him to his religion, not interfering with him therein]. (S, Msb, K, voce دَيَّنْتُهُ.) وَكِيلٌ A witness; syn. شَهِيدٌ. (Jel, ii. 66; and iv. 169.) === A commissioned agent; a factor; a deputy. وَكَّلَهُ بِشَىْءٍ 2 He appointed him, or intrusted him, as his commissioned agent, factor, or deputy, with the management, or disposal, of a thing. === وَكَّلْتُ بِفُلَانٍ I associated a وَكِيل [or factor, &c.] with such a one. (T in art. بِ.) وُكَلَةٌ : see وَكَلٌ
